sources.notes: Official Educational Round 186 editorial page; used with the provided official solution code to confirm the event sweep and ordered-set greedy.

reference_candidates.notes: 洛谷题解候选来源；只有实际参考后才移动到 sources。

annotation.summary: Maximize the number/value of New Year gifts chosen under time/order constraints and a limited upgrade budget.
annotation.constraints: Gift events are processed by coordinate/order, and dynamic choices require deletion of the currently most expensive optional upgrade.
annotation.core_idea: Sort all gift-related events by their position/time and sweep them in reverse. Optional gifts contribute an upgrade cost delta into an ordered multiset. When a forced checkpoint is met, take one available gift by removing the largest currently usable delta; this maximizes the number of forced gifts handled before spending the residual budget. After all checkpoints, greedily buy remaining optional deltas in increasing cost while budget allows.
annotation.complexity: O(n log n) from sorting and ordered multiset operations.
tricks: choose largest for forced slots, cheapest for leftover budget

solution_variants.name: main
solution_variants.summary: Sweep events in reverse, maintain available gift deltas in an ordered multiset, and combine forced picks with a final cheapest-first pass.

tags.evidence: The solution makes local extremal choices: largest currently available delta for forced events and smallest remaining deltas for budget fill.
The sweep needs to insert costs and repeatedly remove/query largest or smallest available values with duplicates.
The event order/deadline structure determines when gifts become available and when a forced selection occurs.
Events and leftover costs are processed in sorted order.
